title: Core Concepts in Modern History
body:
Industrialisation, nationalism, imperialism, world war, decolonisation, and globalisation are the anchor concepts of modern history. Each one describes a force that reshaped how power worked.

Modern historians also pay attention to class, race, gender, migration, and technology because these categories explain who benefitted from the modern order and who paid the costs.

The best modern history moves between the local and the global: factories, parliaments, colonies, households, and international institutions all matter at once.

A single event rarely tells the whole story; the structure around it usually matters more.
furtherReading: The Age of Empire by Eric Hobsbawm
A People’s History of the World by Chris Harman
